input.headline: Meta alerts young Australians to download their data before a social media ban

raw.headline: Meta alerts young Australians to download their data before a social media ban

winner: Meta warned Australian teens that if they don’t download their social media data soon, they’ll just have to remember all those embarrassing group chats from memory. No notes allowed—just pure, unfiltered nostalgia.

candidates.text: Why did Meta send me an email saying “download your data now”? I thought they meant my meme collection—turns out they just wanted to see how fast I could unload my entire life before the government shut down my phone.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on data download, specific reference to government action, concise and natural humor"}

candidates.text: Meta said "download your data" before the ban, but I think they meant "meta-data"—because nothing says "I'm about to get banned" like asking you to download your life story in 140-character chunks.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on 'meta-data,' concise, specific, and ties humor to the headline's context with natural wordplay."}

candidates.text: Meta’s latest PR stunt was so aggressive, even my kid’s iPad said, 'Download your data or we’re cutting you off—just kidding! That’s just how downloads work now.'
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Funny, concise, and uses a relatable scenario with a clever twist involving the iPad's sarcastic comment."}

candidates.text: Meta warned Australian teens that if they don’t download their social media data soon, they’ll just have to remember all those embarrassing group chats from memory. No notes allowed—just pure, unfiltered nostalgia.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on data download warnings, specific to Australian teens, concise and uses relatable humor about group chats and nostalgia."}

candidates.text: What kind of social media is safe now that Australia is banning it? Only the ones with no data—like ghosts on Twitter!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative with the ghost metaphor but lacks clarity and misses the specific context of Meta's alert and data download."}
